task.title: Network Connectivity Verification
task.description: Perform passive monitoring of network interface status and routing table integrity to assess internet connectivity without altering system state.

solution.approach: Identify active network interfaces and inspect current routing table entries to understand the baseline configuration. Collect interface flags (UP, RUNNING), IP addresses, and gateway information without modifying any settings.
solution.algorithm: Use system calls or CLI tools such as 'ip addr show' and 'ip route show' to retrieve interface status and routing table data. Parse output to extract key fields like interface name, state, IP address, and default gateway.
solution.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of network interfaces and routes.

solution.approach: Define a passive monitoring strategy that periodically samples network interface status and routing table without injecting packets or changing configurations. Design checks for interface operstate consistency, presence of default route, and absence of unexpected route changes.
solution.algorithm: Implement polling logic with configurable interval (e.g., every 30 seconds). Store baseline snapshot during first run; compare subsequent snapshots using hash comparison or field-level diffing.
solution.complexity: O(1) per check due to fixed-size data structures, but O(m) memory for storing historical state where m is number of monitored entries.

solution.approach: Execute the designed monitoring loop: capture current interface and route states, compare against baseline, log deviations, and maintain continuous observation in read-only mode.
solution.algorithm: Script using shell commands wrapped in a loop with sleep intervals. Redirect logs to syslog or file. Ensure no outbound traffic is generated—only parsing existing kernel state.
solution.complexity: O(k) per iteration where k is total number of interfaces and routes.

solution.approach: Verify that the monitoring process does not alter system state by confirming zero packet transmission and validating only read operations on /proc/net/ files or equivalent kernel interfaces.
solution.algorithm: Run tcpdump or eBPF-based observer in parallel to confirm absence of new outgoing packets from the monitoring tool. Also validate that all accessed paths are read-only (e.g., /sys/class/net/, /proc/net/route).
solution.complexity: O(p) where p is number of packets observed over monitoring period.

solution.approach: Identify active network interfaces and inspect current routing table entries to understand the baseline configuration. Collect interface flags (UP, RUNNING), IP addresses, and gateway information without modifying any settings.
solution.algorithm: Use system calls or CLI tools such as 'ip addr show' and 'ip route show' to retrieve interface status and routing table data. Parse output to extract key fields like interface name, state, IP address, and default gateway.
solution.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of network interfaces and routes.
